steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Study 1 establishes that genetic similarity between breeds explains behavioral similarity in trainability. Study 2 finds bulldogs and French bulldogs show similar trainability. The question asks what logically follows from combining both findings.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "bulldogs and French bulldogs show a greater tendency toward trainability than most other dog breeds do.": Introduces breeds not discussed — unsupported by the evidence. ✗
  • "individual bulldogs likely display higher levels of trainability than individual French bulldogs.": Study 2 shows similar trainability, not dissimilar. ✗
  • "their similarities with respect to that behavior could result from a shared aspect of their genetics.": Directly applies Study 1's framework to Study 2's finding — similar trainability suggests shared genetics. ✓
  • "the two breeds will likely become less genetically similar over time.": Neither study addresses future change. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
